today_news.title: ChatGPT still leads the chatbot market but its dominance is slipping as Google's Gemini gains ground
today_news.summary: ChatGPT continues to dominate the chatbot market, but its lead is shrinking. According to new Similarweb figures, OpenAI's chatbot has fallen from 75.7 to 61.7 percent market share in just twelve months. The big winner: Google Gemini, which has quadrupled its share from 5.7 to 24.4 percent.

today_news.title: Grok 4.20 trails Gemini and GPT-5.4 by a wide margin but sets a new record for not hallucinating
today_news.summary: xAI's Grok 4.20 is cheap, fast, and hallucinates less than any other tested model, but it can't keep up with the top tier in benchmarks.

today_news.title: The Download: Early adopters cash in on China’s OpenClaw craze, and US batteries slump

today_news.title: Pragmatic by design: Engineering AI for the real world
today_news.summary: The impact of artificial intelligence extends far beyond the digital world and into our everyday lives, across the cars we drive, the appliances in our homes, and medical devices that keep people alive. More and more, product engineers are turning to AI to enhance, validate, and streamline the desig...

today_news.title: Claude can now create interactive charts and visualizations directly in chat
today_news.summary: Anthropic has launched a new beta feature for its AI chatbot Claude: the ability to generate interactive diagrams, charts, and visualizations directly within the conversation.

today_news.title: Copilot Health marks Microsoft's entry into the AI health race alongside OpenAI and Anthropic
today_news.summary: Microsoft is launching Copilot Health, an AI health assistant that pulls data from wearables, medical records, and lab results to deliver personalized health advice. Long term, the company says it's working toward "medical superintelligence."

today_news.title: A defense official reveals how AI chatbots could be used for targeting decisions
today_news.summary: The US military might use generative AI systems to rank lists of targets and make recommendations—which would be vetted by humans—about which to strike first, according to a Defense Department official with knowledge of the matter. The disclosure about how the military may use AI chatbots comes as t...

today_news.title: Brutal times for the US battery industry
today_news.summary: Just a few years ago, the battery industry was hot, hot, hot. There was a seemingly infinite number of companies popping up, with shiny new chemistries and massive fundraising rounds. today_tech.title: Can LLMs Help Localize Fake Words in Partially Fake Speech?
today_tech.summary: Abstract: Large language models (LLMs), trained on large-scale text, have recently attracted significant attention for their strong performance across many tasks. today_tech.title: A Simple Efficiency Incremental Learning Framework via Vision-Language Model with Nonlinear Multi-Adapters
today_tech.summary: Abstract: Incremental Learning (IL) aims to learn new tasks while preserving previously acquired knowledge. today_tech.title: Summarize Before You Speak with ARACH: A Training-Free Inference-Time Plug-In for Enhancing LLMs via Global Attention Reallocation
today_tech.summary: Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) achieve remarkable performance, yet further gains often require costly training. today_tech.title: Senna-2: Aligning VLM and End-to-End Driving Policy for Consistent Decision Making and Planning
today_tech.summary: Abstract: Vision-language models (VLMs) enhance the planning capability of end-to-end (E2E) driving policy by leveraging high-level semantic reasoning. today_tech.title: PACED: Distillation at the Frontier of Student Competence
today_tech.summary: Abstract: Standard LLM distillation wastes compute on two fronts: problems the student has already mastered (near-zero gradients) and problems far beyond its reach (incoherent gradients that erode existing capabilities).
